page.title: Taste of Tea & Horse Road Ecotour

media.altText: South Gate Square of Lijiang Old Town with traditional Naxi architecture

sections.subtitle: Roasted tea with the last caravaneer
sections.text: A few small cups of roasted tea, a piece of baba bread, and a Zhongyi Bazaar stall you won't find on your own. We guide you from the South Gate through the Tea & Horse Road Museum, sit you down with the last caravaneer for a fire-pit tea, then end at Zhongyi Bazaar for chickpea jelly, yak butter tea and other historic delicacies. You'll come home smelling faintly of charcoal-roasted tea, and with a caravaneer story lingering in your head.

items.title: What's Special
items.subtitle: Tea ceremony, caravaneer stories, bazaar snacks
items.highlight: A guided tasting trail through ancient Tea & Horse Road

items.title: Duration
items.subtitle: Half-day
items.highlight: Morning: 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM | Afternoon: 2:00 PM to 6:00 PM

items.title: Location
items.subtitle: Lijiang Old Town
items.highlight: Lijiang Old Town UNESCO World Heritage Site | Altitude 2,400m

items.title: Hidden Square
items.subtitle: South Gate Square
items.text: Step into Lijiang's Old Town at South Gate Square, a hidden historic market place where old handicrafts line the plaza.

image.altText: South Gate Square of Lijiang Old Town with Naxi architecture

items.title: Tea & Horse Road Museum
items.subtitle: Trade Route History
items.text: Trace centuries of caravaneer trade between Yunnan and Tibet through artifacts, maps, and old photographs inside this small but rich museum.

image.altText: Museum exhibit about the Tea and Horse Road trade route

items.title: Ancient Roasted Tea Ceremony
items.subtitle: Caravaneer Tradition
items.text: Watch tea leaves dance over a charcoal brazier, then sip the smoky, earthy brew that once fueled traders on long and strenuous mountain crossings.

image.altText: Traditional roasted tea ceremony over a charcoal brazier

items.title: The Last Caravaneer
items.subtitle: First-Hand Stories
items.text: Sit with the legendary last caravaneer and hear first-hand what life was like on the Tea & Horse Road — the dangers, friendships, and mountain passes.

image.altText: Portrait of the last caravaneer of the Tea and Horse Road

items.title: Zhongyi Bazaar
items.subtitle: Zhongyi Market
items.text: Wander Lijiang's most authentic bazaar since the Qing Dynasty: mountain vegetables, dangerous mushrooms, yak cheese, and seasonal fruits.

image.altText: Zhongyi local market stalls with mountain produce

items.title: Traditional Naxi Snacks
items.subtitle: Many Local Snacks
items.text: Sample chickpea jelly, fried baba bread, griddled cheese, and sweet rose-flavored pastries — each with a story rooted in mountain agriculture and Naxi grandmothers.

image.altText: Selection of traditional Naxi and Yunnan street snacks

items.title: Tibetan Yak Butter Tea
items.subtitle: Caravaneer Sustenance
items.text: Sip the rich, salty yak butter tea that sustained Tibetan traders for centuries. Frothy, smoky, and an acquired taste that connects you to the high caravans.

image.altText: Bowl of traditional Tibetan yak butter tea

items.title: Deep Fried Insects
items.subtitle: Crunchy Crickets
items.text: For the adventurous: crispy crickets, silkworm pupae, bamboo worms. Optional, and we promise the look is more dramatic than the taste.

image.altText: Skewers of deep fried insects at a Yunnan market

items.title: Yunnan Fruits & Vegetables
items.subtitle: Unique Local Harvest
items.text: Discover Yunnan's mountain biodiversity: snake fruits, Buddha's hand citron, tree tomatoes, and wild mushrooms in season. The bazaar is a living agricultural museum.

image.altText: Colorful local mountain fruits and vegetables at the bazaar

activities: Meet your guide at your Lijiang hotel and walk together into the heart of the Old Town, starting at the historic South Gate Square. From there, visit the Tea & Horse Road Museum to trace the ancient trade route, then sit down with the last caravaneer for a traditional roasted tea ceremony. Wander Zhongyi Bazaar tasting local snacks, deep-fried insects, mountain fruits, and frothy yak butter tea before strolling back through the Old Town to your hotel.

contactNote.text: For groups of 1 person or more than 6 people

included.text: English-Speaking Coordinator/Guide (Lynne Lyu or Lukasz Madrzynski), Local Caravaneer Guide, Entrance Fees to Tea & Horse Road Museum, Traditional Roasted Tea Ceremony, All Food Tastings, Outdoor Travel Insurance.
included.notIncluded: Personal Expenses (Souvenirs, etc.), Voluntary Gratuities for the Guide
included.meetingPoint: Pickup: 9:00 AM (morning tour) or 2:00 PM (afternoon tour) at your Lijiang hotel | Return: Approximately 1:00 PM or 6:00 PM to your Lijiang hotel

sections.subtitle: Click on a question to reveal the answer

items.question: What should I bring?
items.answer: Comfortable walking shoes, layers for changing temperatures, sun protection, umbrella and/or raincoat, a water bottle, and your camera. Detailed packing list provided upon booking.

items.question: Is this suitable for children?
items.answer: Yes! This tour is family-friendly. Children under 12 should be accompanied by an adult. The slow pace and 2-3 km walk are achievable for most ages. Note that some market snacks (spicy, fried) may be too strong for very young children.

items.question: What's the cancellation policy?
items.answer: Full refund if cancelled 7+ days before the tour. 50% refund for 3-6 days. No refund within 48 hours. Weather contingencies always have alternatives.

items.question: What happens if it rains?
items.answer: The tour continues during light rain. For heavy storms we'll adjust to spend more time indoors (covered sections of bazaar, museum and caravaneer's courtyard house).

items.question: Are there any physical requirements?
items.answer: Basic mobility is enough. You'll walk 2-3 km on flat cobblestone streets at a slow pace, with frequent stops for tasting and storytelling. Suitable for most fitness levels.

items.question: How do I get to the meeting point?
items.answer: We provide pickup from any hotel in Lijiang Old Town or the surrounding area. If you're staying outside Lijiang, please contact us to arrange an alternative meeting point.

items.question: What language do the guides speak?
items.answer: We are fluent in English, Polish and Chinese.

items.question: How does this trip support conservation and sustainable development?
items.answer: Each booking directly supports local Naxi families, the last caravaneer and Tea & Horse Museum. We support small market vendors and traditional tea masters, ensuring your fees stay in the Old Town. Our small-group format minimizes impact on this fragile UNESCO site while maximizing benefit to its living culture.

items.question: Do I have to eat the insects?
items.answer: No, you don't. The deep-fried insects are an optional tasting. You're welcome to skip them and try the many other snacks on offer — there's plenty for every palate.

title.text: Ready to Taste Lijiang's Old Town?

seo.metaTitle: Tea & Horse Road Tour | Yunnan Culinary Walking Experience in Lijiang
seo.metaDescription: Taste the ancient Tea & Horse Road on this half-day Yunnan culinary walking tour in Lijiang. Sip traditional roasted tea, try Naxi snacks, and explore cobblestone lanes with a local guide. Small-group Yunnan eco tour.---
